text: <par>Apatani preserves reflexes of both the voiceless and voiced allofams of this etymon (<latinform>pù</latinform> 'bloom'; <latinform>bu</latinform> 'give birth').</par>

<par>In this interesting collocation, the first word <latinform>à-diŋ</latinform> means 'head', while the second <latinform>dim-pu</latinform> means 'white hair' (in <latinform>dim-pu</latinform>, note the assimilation of the final of the first syllable to the initial of the second syllable). A painfully literal meaning of this collocation would be 'white-haired head'.</par>
<par>Is this Nbp or 'resin'? Check Apatani 'tooth.' Maybe goes with Ahraing Khumi <latinform>ho²sətaɛ</latinform> (sp?) 'gums.'</par>
<par>Lit. "hip-eye". Cf. the semantic association arising from the rounded nature of the hip joint.</par>
<par>The final nasal in this Damu form is similar to those in Rongmei, Thado, and Mzieme.</par>
